---
title: "Rejection Rate Analysis"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(here)
library(car)
library(viridis)
library(effectsize)
```

## Load Data

```{r load volume data}
# Read the rejected_cattle tab of the Excel file.
port_volume <- read_xlsx(
	here("data", "raw", "cattle port data.xlsx"),
	sheet = "volume_cattle"
)

glimpse(port_volume)
```

```{r load and simplify rejection reasons}
rejections <- read_xlsx(
	here("data", "raw", "cattle port data.xlsx"),
	sheet = "rejected_cattle"
)

# Filter to only include rows where elevated_nws_risk is "yes", then group by port and year, and sum the volume of rejections.
rejections <- rejections |>
    filter(elevated_nws_risk == "yes") |>
    group_by(port, year) |>
    summarise(rejections = sum(volume, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# filter out san luis which never had any shipments
rejections <- rejections |>
    filter(port != "san_luis")

glimpse(rejections)
```

## Merge volume and rejection data

```{r merge data}
# Merge the volume and rejection data by port and year.
rejections_volume <- rejections %>%
    left_join(port_volume, by = c("port", "year"))

rejections_volume <- rejections_volume %>%
    mutate(rejection_rate = rejections / volume)

rejections_volume <- rejections_volume %>%
    mutate(across(c(port, year), as.factor))

glimpse(rejections_volume)
```

## View rejection rates by port (factor) and year (x-axis)

```{r plot rejection rates}
rejections_volume <- rejections_volume %>%
    mutate(log1p_rejection_rate = log1p(rejection_rate))

rej_rate_plot <- ggplot(rejections_volume, aes(x = year, y = rejection_rate, color = port)) +
    geom_boxplot(aes(group = year)) +
    geom_line() +
    geom_point() +
    labs(title = "Rejection Rate by Port and Year", x = "Year", y = "Fourth Root of Rejection Rate") +
    theme_minimal()

rej_rate_plot <- rej_rate_plot +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "D")

rej_rate_fig <- here("outputs", "figures", "rejection_rate.png")
ggsave(
	filename = rej_rate_fig,
	plot = rej_rate_plot,
	width = 10,
	height = 7,
	dpi = 150
)

cat("Rejection rate figure saved to:", rej_rate_fig, "\n")
knitr::include_graphics(rej_rate_fig)
```


## Check ANOVA Assumptions

```{r check-anova-assumptions}
# Check normality of rejection_rate
shapiro_test <- shapiro.test(rejections_volume$rejection_rate)
cat("Shapiro-Wilk normality test p-value:", shapiro_test$p.value, "\n")
# non-normal

#4th root transformation
rejections_volume <- rejections_volume %>%
    mutate(rejection_rate_4th_root = sqrt(sqrt(rejection_rate)))
shapiro_test <- shapiro.test(rejections_volume$rejection_rate_4th_root)
cat("Shapiro-Wilk normality test p-value:", shapiro_test$p.value, "\n")
#this is normal; can use. - the previous data would probably have normally distributed residuals, which is the real test, but some high points in 2023 may have led astray; this is transformation will be preferable from an ANOVA stand point.

# Check homogeneity of variances
levene_test <- car::leveneTest(rejection_rate_4th_root ~ port, data = rejections_volume)
cat("Levene's test for homogeneity of variances p-value:", levene_test$`Pr(>F)`[1], "\n")

# Save the visualization of ANOVA assumptions
assumptions_fig <- here("outputs", "figures", "anova_assumptions.png")

png(assumptions_fig, width = 1600, height = 800, res = 150)
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
hist(rejections_volume$rejection_rate_4th_root, main = "Histogram of Rejection Rate (4th Root)", xlab = "Rejection Rate (4th Root)")
qqnorm(rejections_volume$rejection_rate_4th_root)
qqline(rejections_volume$rejection_rate_4th_root, col = "red")
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
dev.off()

cat("ANOVA assumptions visualization saved to:", assumptions_fig, "\n")
```

## Conduct ANOVA

```{r conduct-anova}
# # Perform ANOVA on rejection_rate_4th_root by port
# anova_result <- aov(rejection_rate_4th_root ~ port + year, data = rejections_volume)
# summary(anova_result)

# # Post-hoc test if ANOVA is significant
# tukey_result_port <- TukeyHSD(anova_result, "port")
# print(tukey_result_port)
# tukey_result_year <- TukeyHSD(anova_result, "year")
# print(tukey_result_year)


# W/ year as a covariate
rejections_volume <- rejections_volume %>%
    mutate(year_numeric = as.numeric(as.character(year)))

fit <- lm(rejection_rate_4th_root ~ port * year_numeric, data = rejections_volume)
anova_result <- aov(fit)
summary(anova_result)

broom::tidy(fit)

TukeyHSD(anova_result, "port")


```

```{r tukey-groupings-plot}
# Perform Tukey's HSD test for port
tukey_port <- TukeyHSD(anova_result, "port")

#Extract Tukey's groupings
  # The multcompLetters4 function needs names from the ANOVA result - not the actual TukeyHSD; so, run the ANOVA again without year as a covariate to get the correct names for the multcompLetters4 function
anova_result_port <- aov(rejection_rate_4th_root ~ port, data = rejections_volume)
  # Now extract the Tukey's groupings using names from the model with just port but the Groupings from the model with year as a covariate
library(multcompView)
tukey_letters <- multcompLetters4(anova_result_port, tukey_port)

tukey_df <- data.frame(
  port = names(tukey_letters$port$Letters),
  group = tukey_letters$port$Letters
)

# Calculate mean rejection rates for each port
mean_effects <- rejections_volume %>%
  group_by(port) %>%
  summarise(mean_rate = mean(rejection_rate_4th_root, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  left_join(tukey_df, by = "port")

# Save the Tukey's groupings plot
tukey_plot_fig <- here("outputs", "figures", "tukey_groupings_port.png")

ggplot(mean_effects, aes(x = reorder(port, mean_rate), y = mean_rate, fill = group)) +
  geom_point(stat = "identity", show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = group), vjust = -0.5) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_rate, ymax = mean_rate), width = 0.2) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Effects of Port with Tukey's Groupings",
    x = "Port",
    y = "Mean Fourth Root of Rejection Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() -> tukey_plot

ggsave(
  filename = tukey_plot_fig,
  plot = tukey_plot,
  width = 10,
  height = 7,
  dpi = 150
)

cat("Tukey's groupings plot saved to:", tukey_plot_fig, "\n")
```

## Mean Effects with 95% CI and Tukey's Groupings

```{r tukey-groupings-errorbar-plot}
# Calculate mean effects and 95% CI for each port
port_ci <- rejections_volume %>%
  group_by(port) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_rate = mean(rejection_rate_4th_root, na.rm = TRUE),
    lower_ci = mean_rate - qt(0.975, df = n() - 1) * sd(rejection_rate_4th_root, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    upper_ci = mean_rate + qt(0.975, df = n() - 1) * sd(rejection_rate_4th_root, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n())
  ) %>%
  left_join(tukey_df, by = "port")

port_ci <- port_ci %>%
  mutate(mean_rate = mean_rate^4) %>%
  mutate(lower_ci = lower_ci^4) %>%
  mutate(upper_ci = upper_ci^4)

# Plot mean effects with 95% CI and Tukey's groupings
tukey_errorbar_plot <- ggplot(port_ci, aes(x = reorder(port, mean_rate), y = mean_rate, fill = group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower_ci, ymax = upper_ci), width = 0.2) +
  geom_text(aes(label = group), vjust = -0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Effects of Port with 95% CI and Tukey's Groupings",
    x = "Port",
    y = "Mean Rejection Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_viridis_d()

# Save the plot
tukey_errorbar_fig <- here("outputs", "figures", "tukey_groupings_port_errorbar.png")

ggsave(
  filename = tukey_errorbar_fig,
  plot = tukey_errorbar_plot,
  width = 10,
  height = 7,
  dpi = 150
)

cat("Tukey's groupings plot with error bars saved to:", tukey_errorbar_fig, "\n")
```

# join rejection rates with tukeys groupings and mean effects for each port
```{r join-rejection-rates}
rejection_rates_with_tukey <- rejections_volume %>%
  group_by(port) %>%
  summarise(mean_rate = mean((rejection_rate_4th_root^4), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  left_join(tukey_df, by = "port")
```

# Save rejection rates

```{r save-rejection-rates}
#save summary of rejection rates by port and year
rejection_rates_csv <- here("outputs", "data", "rejection_rates.csv")
rejection_rates_rds <- here("outputs", "data", "rejection_rates.rds")

write_csv(rejections_volume, rejection_rates_csv)
saveRDS(rejections_volume, rejection_rates_rds)

cat("Rejection rates data saved to:", rejection_rates_csv, "and", rejection_rates_rds, "\n")

# Save mean rejection rates with Tukey's groupings
mean_rejection_rates_csv <- here("outputs", "data", "mean_rejection_rates_with_tukey.csv")
mean_rejection_rates_rds <- here("outputs", "data", "mean_rejection_rates_with_tukey.rds")

write_csv(rejection_rates_with_tukey, mean_rejection_rates_csv)
saveRDS(rejection_rates_with_tukey, mean_rejection_rates_rds)

cat("Mean rejection rates with Tukey's groupings saved to:", mean_rejection_rates_csv, "and", mean_rejection_rates_rds, "\n")
```
